feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Extend Datasphere Business content package.
entry.content:
Hello,
In BW you could make a copy of the Business content to extend it with more fields.
In the case of SAP Datasphere, what would be the recommendation? If we want to use the Sales package of the Business content, what would be the best practice to extend it?
I understand that if we extend the contents of the package directly, we would lose the changes in the case of a new import, right?
Thank you very much.

entry.title: Dynamically set default DATE value for input parameter in Datasphere or SAC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

  Is it possible to dynamically set default value of DATE variable for previous day(CURRENT_DATE - 1) using INPUT PARAMETER(Datasphere) or Variable(SAC) ?

Thanks a lot

entry.title: Getting Column name inconsistency error in Dataspehre
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I"m getting below error, when trying to deploy the subsequent flow:

Review the following errors:
"DF_Table1" - Table DF_Table1 inconsistent: Column "Month" in deployed model but not on DB,
Correlation ID: bd56da16-3216-4994-4fa0-73d14024a86

entry.title: How to Identify CDS Views as Alternatives to Classic Extractors for SAP Datasphere Integration
entry.content:
Dear Datasphere Experts

I’m currently working on integrating SAP S/4HANA with SAP Datasphere using ODP, as recommended by SAP. I understand that instead of the classic extractors (e.g., 2LIS_11_VAHDR, 0FI_GL_4), it’s now best practice to leverage CDS views.

Could someone guide me on how to:

Identify the relevant CDS views that correspond to these classic extractors?
Validate if these CDS views are ODP-enabled for extraction to SAP Datasphere?
Handle cases where no direct CDS view is available for a classic extractor?
Any tips, examples, or SAP resources (e.g., notes, documentation) would be greatly appreciated, especially for the specific examples like 2LIS_11_VAHDR and 0FI_GL_4.

Thank you for your insights

Priyanka

entry.title: Compatibility of SAP ECC version 6 EHP 5 with SAP Datasphere
entry.content: I would like to know if SAP Datasphere can be integrated with SAP ECC version 6.0 EHP 5

entry.title: Datasphere Replication flow - Delta load of LO or SAPI extractor from ECC
entry.content:
Dear Datasphere Experts,

This pertains to the extraction of LO extractors (2LIS*) from ECC to a third-party target, such as AWS, using a "replication flow" facilitated by premium outbound connectivity.

According to SAP documentation, only an initial load is supported when the source object lacks a primary key for ODP artifacts.

Reference: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/c8a54ee704e94e15926551293243fd1d/2267a9f6a5724ec0ae5632db4f9f70e5.html

Here are my questions:

  1. What are the possible workarounds for this limitation, considering LO extractors (2LIS*) are widely used across organizations, and delta functionality is essential?
  2. Is there a plan in SAP's roadmap (I couldn’t locate one) to provide delta functionality for all standard and custom extractions without primary key?

This is a common requirement as It work perfectly in SAP BW, and I believe it warrants attention. I would appreciate your guidance.

BR,

Yatendra

entry.title: SAP Datasphere replication flow projection filter for null/ Blank values.
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,
What is expression in Projection filter in SAP Datasphere replication flow for null/ Blank values. Any clue would be helpful.
Best Regards
Arvind

entry.title: Is there any possibility to convert a Hana Graphical Calculation view to SAP DataSphere view?
entry.content:
I have a new mission that is figure out a way to convert Hana Graphical view to SAP DataSphere view.

I did not find any doc or even comment about this procedure, so does any one had issue like this or have heard something that can help me. 

 

Thanks in Advance

entry.title: DAC not working in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi  All 

 

I been following this blog 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/introducing-new-type-of-data-access-control-quot-operator-and-value-quot/bc-p/13946785#M177241

I recreated the blog and my results are all good except for product_group = 2. 

my view

my DAC table 

 

my actual table 

what could be the issue , it only does not work for product_group = 2 ( it works for all other product groups)

entry.title: Migrating from SAP BW to Datasphere: Using 2LIS_13_VDHDR and 2LIS_13_VDITM Data Sources for Delta
entry.content:
Hello Community,

We are currently using SAP BW 7.3 and are in the process of transitioning to SAP Datasphere. However, we are not planning to use the Data Bridge in our migration strategy.

In our current setup, the source system for BW is SAP ECC. Going forward, the source system for SAP Datasphere will be SAP S/4HANA.

at present In BW, we use the standard InfoCube 0SD_C03 to extract sales information. The data flows into this InfoCube through standard extractors 2LIS_13_VDHDR and 2LIS_13_VDITM, which asupports the delta-extraction from SAP ECC to BW.

My questions are as follows:

  1. Can the same extractors (2LIS_13_VDHDR and 2LIS_13_VDITM) be used directly in SAP Datasphere to extract delta records from SAP S/4HANA? if yes.. how to enable?
  2. Does SAP Datasphere provide any standard views or models that replicate the Exact functionality/fields of the mentioned extractors or InfoCube 0SD_C03?

I would appreciate guidance on whether there are standard approaches or recommendations for implementing such delta-supported scenarios in SAP Datasphere, especially when transitioning from BW.

Thank you.

SKS

entry.title: SAP DataSphere and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
HI Team,

Can you share the learning paths for SAP Datasphere and SAC?

Regards,

entry.title: Help with reporting layout in SAP Datasphere/ Analytics Cloud/ Web Intelligence
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am using an existing model from SAP datasphere and consuming it in SAP Analytics cloud. In Datasphere I have created several Restricted measures. 

The Raw data which I am working on is as follows:

Below is the list of all Dimensions and Measures used in the model.

Below is the list of all Restricted Measures used in the model.

In the above attachment, the measures indicated with a Red Dot represent Restricted Measures in SAP Datasphere. 

The Expressions used in each of the above Restricted Measures are as follows:

  • 2023 Full: Year = '2023'
  • 2023 Nov: Year = '2023' AND Month = '11'
  • 2024 Full: Year = '2024'
  • 2024-Nov: Year = '2024' AND Month = '11'

Likewise, in SAP Analytics cloud I have also created several dimension groups to be used along with the Restricted measures which were created in SAP datasphere.

The formula used in each of the above Calculated Dimensions include the following:

  • Cost: IF([d/"DS_Structure_Test":GL].[p/GL] = ("GL3")and[d/"DS_Structure_Test":"Doc type"].[p/Doc type]=("D5"),"Cost" ,"Others")
  • Expense: IF([d/"DS_Structure_Test":GL].[p/GL] = ("GL2")and[d/"DS_Structure_Test":"Doc type"].[p/Doc type]=("D2","D4"),"Expense" ,"Others")
  • Income1: IF([d/"DS_Structure_Test":GL].[p/GL] = ("GL1")and[d/"DS_Structure_Test":"Doc type"].[p/Doc type]=("D1","D2"),"Income" ,"Others")

In a nutshell, I was trying to group several dimensions together based on a certain condition. If the condition is true, it displays the name of the calculated dimension, else it displays "Others". 

Below is the output of the Calculated Dimensions in SAP Analytics Cloud:

The goal is to exclude the rows with "Others" in the table.

I tried using filters to the above table to exclude "Others" and below is the result:

 

Below is the expected output:

 

In SAP BW, the above output is achieved using Structures (Rows-Restricted Characteristics and columns-Restricted Key Figures) in a BeX query, but now the goal is to get the output in the same format (Rows down and Columns across) as mentioned above in SAP Datasphere/ Analytics Cloud / Web Intelligence.

 

Would be great if someone has encountered the above scenario and has achieved a solution.

Thanks in advance.

Regards, 

Saifullah Sharieef

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - Business Content MM
entry.content:
Hello experts,
Could someone tell me if there is a SAP Datasphere Business Content package for the MM module, or at least if there is something pre-built for the Material.

Regards,
Leonel

entry.title: Question about replication flow in initial mode with the status "Retrying"
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a question about replication flow in "Initial" mode.

I have put some replication flow (all in "initial") in a task chain. When i run the task for the first time, the data loading works fine, but when i retry to run the same task chain to update my data, some replication flow are in status "Running (Retrying Objects)" (even if the replication is not a delta).I don't understant why.

Datapshere try to run the loading but the same message appears :"The source connector has accepted the initial load partitioning request. The next attempt will be after ..."

Do you know why init replication flow still retrying to load data ? (is not a delta)

Do you already have the same issue ? 

Thanks for the help,

Best regards

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Datasphere CLI - Create Object with --input and node.js
entry.content:
Hello community,

I am trying to create an object with the Datasphere CLI with node.js, but I cannot figure out the correct syntax for the command.

As described in the documentation for the CLI there is the paramater "--input" to use a stringified-json to create an object (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/d0ecd6f297ac40249072a44df0549c1a/6f5c65f209004751aa48f9682ee2ec45.html?locale=en-US#create-objects). Unfortunately I couldn't find any example explaining the syntax of the used stringified-json.

I have successfully created an object with the parameter "--file-path" and a JSON file. But when I stringify this file in node.js and try the command with this stringified JSON, I get the error message "Failed to obtain an object name from the provided CSN code".

I have also tried to escape the quotation marks in the JSON file with "\" and with "`".

The code with escaped quotation marks looks like this: 

 

const options4 = {     
"--space": "SP_IB_006",     
"--technical-name": "T3",     
"--input": "{\"definitions\":{\"T3\":{\"kind\":\"entity\",\"@EndUserText.label\":\"T3\",\"@ObjectModel.modelingPattern\":{\"#\":\"DATA_STRUCTURE\"},\"@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities\":[{\"#\":\"DATA_STRUCTURE\"}],\"elements\":{\"ID\":{\"@EndUserText.label\":\"ID\",\"type\":\"cds.String\",\"length\":100,\"key\":true,\"notNull\":true},\"Name\":{\"@EndUserText.label\":\"Name\",\"type\":\"cds.String\",\"length\":100}}}}}",     
"--host": "<HOST_OF_DSP>",       
"--passcode": "",         
};       

try {      
const retrieveCommand = await commands2["objects local-tables create"](options4);   
} catch (err) {    
console.log(err);   
}

 

 

In powershell I could successfully create the object by using the following command:

 

datasphere objects local-tables create --space SP_IB_006 --technical-name T3 --input "{`"definitions`":{`"T3`":{`"kind`":`"entity`",`"@EndUserText.label`":`"T3`",`"@ObjectModel.modelingPattern`":{`"#`":`"DATA_STRUCTURE`"},`"@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities`":[{`"#`":`"DATA_STRUCTURE`"}],`"elements`":{`"ID`":{`"@EndUserText.label`":`"ID`",`"type`":`"cds.String`",`"length`":100,`"key`":true,`"notNull`":true},`"Name`":{`"@EndUserText.label`":`"Name`",`"type`":`"cds.String`",`"length`":100}}}}}"

 

 

Does anyone know, how to use the "--input"-paramater in node.js correctly?

Best Regards,
Björn

entry.title: Eclipse BW Bridge Project: Logon to the service instance has failed.
entry.content:
Hello,

I have the latest Eclipse version (2024-12) installed with the latest ADT and MDT. I try to add a BW bridge project by extracting the URL from the service key. I get prompted to login and do so successfully on an external browser window. As soon as I do, I get an error in Eclipse "Logon to the service instance has failed." I can't find anything related to it elsewhere. Has anyone come across it?

entry.title: Does datasphere work good with ECC or should it be with S4/Hana
entry.content:
Hello

Gurus,

I would like to know that if SAP Datasphere with work fine with ECC 6.0 as far as performance and all the features are concerned or Is it best delivered in when connected with S4/HANA.

We have ECC 6.0 and our roadmap to implement S4 in 2027 but we are trying to do PoC  on Datasphere which is connected with ECC and I am just wondering what are the advantages or pros & cons of datasphere connected with ECC or should we wait for S4 and then implement datasphere.

Thanks for your advice in advance.

Regards

entry.title: SAP DATASPHERE HANDSON Course materials
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Please share the course materials Handson for SAP Datasphere

Thanks,

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Integration with Board
entry.content:
Hi there,

has anyone yet tried to integrate Datasphere data with Board? We have tried to connect the two insatnces via API's but this unfortunately did not work. If connection does not work an automated way to import/export data between the two instances would also be very helpful.

Many thanks in advance,

Moritz

entry.title: How to find data source, extractor, CDS views for time info object
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I like to know how do I find the data sources, extractors and CDS views which has any time characteristics. In other words I like to know that my time related info objects such as 0calmonth or 0calweek or 0fiscper info objects how do I know in which data sources, extractors and CDS views are using these info objects.

If I go to "where used-list" I dont see this information but only ADSO, Queries transformations etc.

I know there is a table name RSOSFIELDMAP which we can use in classic BW, t-code SE 11 to see the mapping from data sources for an object but this t-code is not working for me in BW 4/HANA.

Do I have to use Eclipse or HANA studio or can I use RSA1 in BW 4/HANA environment.

Thanks and much appreciated.

Regards
